title: lupinus microcarpus var. horizontalis
intro: Lupinus microcarpus var. horizontalis, also known as the horizontalistic lupine or sand lupine, is a variety of the annual lupine plant that grows horizontally along the ground, typically found in sandy or dry areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic agent. The health benefits of Lupinus microcarpus var. horizontalis include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve kidney function, and reduce inflammation, making it a valuable herb for treating conditions such as arthritis and kidney stones. Herbal preparations of Lupinus microcarpus var. horizontalis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to extract its medicinal compounds and provide relief from various health issues.
